properties.name: Serra da Estrela (Manteigas / Covilhã / Guarda)
properties.description: Validation AOI centered on the August 2022 Serra da Estrela fire — Portugal's largest single fire in 50 years, ~25,000 ha burned, primarily inside the Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela. Spans Manteigas, Covilhã, Guarda and Celorico da Beira municipalities. Copernicus EMS activation EMSR618 provides independent perimeter reference. Promoted from alt_serra_da_estrela.geojson (2026-06-16) for WU-18 widened validation; bbox expanded west and south (vs the alt file's [-7.727, 40.215, -7.373, 40.485]) to include Covilhã urban area and fuller western slope, making the AOI clearly larger than the pilot (~1678 km2 vs pilot ~902 km2). Source: ICNF Áreas Ardidas (https://www.icnf.pt/florestas/gfr/gfrint/ifiarf); Copernicus EMSR618 (https://emergency.copernicus.eu/mapping/list-of-components/EMSR618, accessed 2026-06-16).
properties.rationale: Strong recent burn signal (2022), clean Copernicus EMS perimeters (EMSR618) as independent ground truth, protected-area and high-mountain regime distinct from the Atlantic-Centro pilot. High-altitude shrub and pine fuel mix exercises different FBFM40 classes. Tests whether the exposure score transfers from low-altitude eucalyptus-dominated landscape to high-mountain mixed-fuel terrain. Bbox expanded (vs alt file) to include Covilhã city to the south (>50,000 residents, dense power grid) and fuller mountain massif west of Manteigas, maximising OSM infrastructure density while retaining the core burn scar.
properties.source: Promoted from data/aoi/alt_serra_da_estrela.geojson with bbox expansion. Original alt bbox: [-7.727, 40.215, -7.373, 40.485]. Expansion rationale: include Covilhã (40.28°N, 7.50°W) and fuller western slope to increase AOI size above pilot and improve OSM critical-infra density. Fire event: ICNF Áreas Ardidas 2022; Copernicus EMS EMSR618.
properties.anchor_fire_source: ICNF Áreas Ardidas 2022 — Serra da Estrela fire; Copernicus EMS EMSR618 (https://emergency.copernicus.eu/mapping/list-of-components/EMSR618, accessed 2026-06-16)
properties.copernicus_ems: EMSR618 — clean independent perimeter ground truth
properties.icnf_coverage: strong — ICNF canonical perimeters from 2022 present and finalised
properties.osm_density: moderate — high-mountain terrain thinner than pilot; Covilhã inclusion improves count
properties.validation_notes: Bbox expanded from alt file. Expansion preserves the core 2022 scar while adding Covilhã (southeast) to boost OSM power-grid density. Verify OSM critical-infra count before declaring OSM density non-trivial per Phase A gate (d). EMSR618 perimeter is the primary burn-label source (use in preference to ICNF if resolution differs).
